transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning Rushil Bhatia. Here is your daily news for Monday, July 20. In today's briefing: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, across india, around the world. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. Spain dethrone Argentina to win FIFA World Cup! Torres delivers extra-time heartbreak to Messi's 10-man side. Spain secured a dramatic FIFA World Cup victory over Argentina. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in extra time, ending the match 1-0. Argentina played with ten men after Enzo Fernandez received a red card. Lionel Messi's final World Cup appearance concluded without a trophy. 'Your tears are our tears, Captain': Heartbroken Messi breaks down after defeat. Spain secured their second World Cup title after defeating Argentina in the 2026 final. Lionel Messi's quest for consecutive titles ended in disappointment following a 0-1 extra-time loss. The Argentine Football Association shared an emotional tribute to their captain after the match. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the contest. 20-0: How Spain utterly owned Argentina in World Cup final and rendered Messi irrelevant. Spain's triumph was built not on moments of brilliance alone but on collective superiority. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled possession, dictated territory and suffocated every avenue Argentina attempted to explore. World Cup finals are often remembered for drama, controversy or moments of individual inspiration. This one will be remembered for something far rarer - complete domination. Oil prices jump 3 percent as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent crude tops $90 per barrel. Oil prices climbed significantly as United States and Iran conflict intensified. Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz faced increasing pressure and disruptions. US military actions continued for a ninth consecutive night against Iran. Bahrain and Kuwait reported fresh missile and drone strikes across the region. Monsoon Session begins today; Oppn set to target Centre over Ram temple row, NEET, E20. Cong can raise demand, can't force govt: Rijiju Stable, but weak: Doctors continue electrolyte therapy for Sonam Wangchuk. Sonam Wangchuk continues his indefinite fast, remaining medically vulnerable and under close hospital observation. Doctors report his vital signs are stable, but blood parameters show borderline abnormalities. A joint medical team from Safdarjung Hospital and AIIMS monitors his condition closely. His wife questioned hospital management and sought a transfer, which the court declined. US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts. A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. After Skyroot, more pvt spacetech startups set to reach for skies with commercial missions. India's private space sector is accelerating after a historic rocket launch. Companies are now preparing for commercial missions and reusable rocket development. Several spacetech startups are planning multiple satellite launches in coming years. This increased launch activity will help Indian satellite firms compete globally. Warmer habitats push frogs and salamanders to change diets, study finds. Warming waters cause tadpoles to adjust their diets for survival. Young amphibians increase feeding rates and shift toward more plant material. This strategy helps them cope with rising energy demands. However, faster growth leads to smaller and weaker bodies. From unpaid electric bills to $300M island: Larry Ellison buys Hawaii property 3x the size of SF. Once grappling with utility bills for his workshop, tech magnate Larry Ellison transformed his fortunes by acquiring 98 percent of a Hawaiian island for a staggering $300 million. Now residing full-time on Lanai, he envisions a luxury destination coupled with a sustainable community. Despite fluctuations in his wealth driven by market trends, Oracle's cloud operations showcase remarkable strength amid ongoing challenges. Heavy security at Jantar Mantar as CJP gears up for Parliament march: Top developments. NEW DELHI: Hundreds of Cockroach Janata Party supporters gathered at Jantar Mantar on Sunday ahead of the party's proposed 'Chalo Sansad' march to Parliament on Monday. 'A disgrace': Argentina star sent off after grabbing Spain player's throat after loss. Spain's 2026 FIFA World Cup celebrations were marred by chaotic post-match scenes after Argentina midfielder Leandro Paredes was sent off following a mass confrontation after the final whistle. The defending champions' frustrations boiled over after their 1-0 extra-time defeat, with Paredes dismissed for his role in the altercation as pundits Gary Neville and Alan Shearer condemned Argentina's conduct.

transcript.title: Bhopal and Madhya Pradesh
transcript.text: Now, news closer to home in Bhopal and across Madhya Pradesh. MP Cabinet Clears UCC Bill, Exempts Tribals; Bill To Be Tabled In Assembly. The Madhya Pradesh Cabinet unanimously approved the Uniform Civil Code Bill, 2026, exempting Scheduled Tribes. Bhopal Court Acquits Man In rupees 20 Lakh Cheque Bounce Appeal, Sets Aside Conviction. A Bhopal District and Sessions Court acquitted a man in a cheque bounce case, overturning his 2025 conviction under the Negotiable Instruments Act. Assembly Business: 5 Departments Account For Highest Pendency Of House-Related Matters. Ahead of the monsoon session, 1,336 Vidhan Sabha matters remain pending across Madhya Pradesh departments. Excise Department Raids Dhabas Across Indore, Registers 50+ Cases. The State Excise Department registered over 50 cases during raids on dhabas across Indore and the Bypass Road for illegal liquor serving and public drinking. Indore Police Lead Massive Car And Bike Rally To Promote Drug-Free Society. Around 750 participants joined Indore Police's IIM Indore Study: Metaverse Workplaces Boost Employee Engagement In Hybrid Offices. An IIM Indore study found that metaverse-enabled workplaces improve employee engagement by promoting social interaction, collaboration and psychological support rather than technology alone.

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. Asian stocks today: South Korea's Kospi declines amid rising crude oil prices, escalating US-Iran war tensions. Asian stocks today: South Korea's Kospi fell 0.74 percent at the opening bell, while the small-cap Kosdaq index declined 1.66 percent . Gift Nifty, US-Iran war, oil prices to Q1 results: 8 key things that changed for Indian stock market over weekend. Gift Nifty was trading around 24,293 level, a discount of nearly 29 points from the Nifty futures' previous close, indicating a weak start for the Indian stock market indices. Crude oil prices jump as US-Iran war escalates; Brent oil tops $90 per barrel. US-Iran war: Brent crude futures rose $2.69, or 4 percent , to $90.79 a barrel, their highest level since June 11. Reliance Industries: O2C shines, but digital commerce push weighs on Q1 earnings. Reliance Industries' O2C business delivered a strong quarter, but retail's digital-commerce investments continued to weigh on profitability. Raja Venkatraman recommends three stocks for 20 July. Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 20 July. Here's his technical outlook and trade strategy. Wangchuk sets three conditions to end hunger strike. Activist Sonam Wangchuk will end his hunger strike if the government accepts accountability for education system failures. Stock recommendations for 20 July from MarketSmith India. MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 20 July. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions. India's long-term outlook stays strong: J.P. Morgan. J.P. SIP investors not reacting to short-term fluctuations, says Amfi chief. Mutual funds are consistently receiving around rupees 30,000 crore every month through SIPs, reflecting investors' confidence in the long-term prospects of the Indian economy and capital markets, says Venkat Nageswar Chalasani, CEO, Amfi Security tight at Jantar Mantar before CJP march. Delhi Police stepped up security around Jantar Mantar ahead of a proposed march to Parliament on the opening day of the Monsoon Session, even as organisers are yet to obtain official permission for the protest. Wall Street Week Ahead: Alphabet, Intel, Tesla earnings take center stage. Although the economic calendar is relatively subdued, several key reports could still influence market sentiment SpaceX targets July 23 for next Starship launch. Elon Musk's SpaceX is targeting July 23 for the 13th Starship test flight after a last-second launch abort last week. Expert view: Selective bets and strong fundamentals amid global chaos, says Vinod Nair. Early Q1 results have also helped reinforce expectations that Indian equities may outperform global peers over the medium term, aided by resilient domestic demand and more reasonable valuations Skyroot shines on debut, now the real test begins. Vikram-1, India's first private orbital rocket, successfully launched carrying multiple payloads. Mcap of five of top-10 most valued firms jumps rupees 1.54 lakh cr; TCS biggest winner. The market valuation of five top firms increased by rupees 1.54 lakh crore last week, led by TCS, amid positive equity trends. The Sensex and Nifty also rose, supported by strong IT earnings and renewed interest in financial stocks. Eng beat Ind 27 runs in 3rd ODI to seal 2-1 series win. England secured a 2-1 series victory after defeating India by twenty-seven runs in the third ODI. Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today , 20 July 2026. Upcoming IPOs: Cube Highways IPO, Xtranet Tech IPO among new issues to open next week; check full list here. Upcoming IPOs: Cube Highways Trust IPO, Xtranet Technologies IPO, Lohia Corp IPO, and Indo Wangchuk to stay at Safdarjung hospital, HC rules. The Delhi High Court ruled Sonam Wangchuk will stay at Safdarjung Hospital for now. HDFC Bank versus ICICI Bank versus Yes Bank: Which stock to buy after Q1 results 2026?. According to Seema Srivastava, Senior Research Analyst at SMC Global Securities, for long-term investors, the private lender is the defensive compounder. 'Couldn't care less': Trump on Iran's peace deal exit. US President Donald Trump on Saturday (local time) said that he "couldn't care less" about Iran's decision to stop abiding by the US-Iran memorandum of understanding (MoU), reiterating that Washington's priority remains preventing Tehran from acquiring a nuclear weapon, Al Jazeera reported. Should you buy Reliance shares after strong Q1 results 2026?. Reliance Industries share price gained 2.50 percent on Friday, ending the session at rupees 1,328.80 apiece. Messi versus Yamal: Veteran genius versus teenage prodigy. Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal face off in a generational football comparison. Q1 results 2026: Infosys to Eternal among companies to declare Q1 results next week; check full list here. Q1 results 2026: Infosys, Eternal, One 97 Communications Paytm, Bajaj Auto, Adani Energy Solutions, Indian Hotels Company, Bharat Petroleum Corporation, Interglobe Aviation, NTPC, and IDFC First Bank are among the marquee companies to report their Q1 results FY27 next week. 12 dead in Poonch and Rajouri after flash floods. At least12 people were killedandseven others went missingafter torrential rain triggered flash floods and landslides inRajouri and Poonchdistricts of Jammu and Kashmir on Sunday, prompting large-scale rescue and relief operations. Indian stock market: How are Sensex and Nifty 50 likely to perform next week amid global tech sell-off?. Indian stock market: The benchmark indices ended the week on a strong note, with the Nifty 50 rising about 0.53 percent to close at 24,334.30, while the Sensex climbed nearly 0.75 percent to settle at 78,151.45 Accenture tweaks salary hikes with cash payout. Accenture is now splitting salary increases equally between base pay and lump-sum payments. Stocks under rupees 100: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three shares to buy on Monday - 20 July 2026. The Indian stock ended the week on a positive note on Friday. Check Sumeet Bagadia's outlook for the market in the coming with, along with the three stocks under rupees 100 that he feels are a buy in current situation. Maharashtra eateries open till 4 am for WC final. The Maharashtra government has allowed restaurants, hotels and eateries to remain open until 4 am on July 20 for the FIFA World Cup 2026 final. Buy or sell: Ganesh Dongre of Anand Rathi recommends 3 stocks to buy on Monday - 20 July 2026. Indian stock market: Amid ongoing Middle East Tension, Ganesh Dongre of Anand Rathi recommends three stocks to buy on Monday. Check top stock picks by the market expert.

transcript.title: Money, tax and personal finance
transcript.text: Now to your money — taxes, savings and personal finance. Investing before an IPO: As NSE, Jio Platforms prepare to list, five early investors reveal the rewards, risks, and patience required. As NSE and Jio Platforms head for blockbuster IPOs, ET Wealth speaks to five early investors. Their journeys reveal the rewards, and the risks. Automatic EPF transfer after changing jobs: Things to check before your balance is moved to the new account. EPFO has introduced automatic PF transfers for eligible members, simplifying the job-switch process. Here are the key things to check before your balance is moved to the new account. Hybrid SIFs: How these specialised investment funds are filling the risk-return gap. Hybrid long-short funds rival debt, arbitrage and hybrid funds, but look beyond the label. Attention taxpayers! Here's what you should do after receiving a notice from the I-T department , Stepwise guide. Income-tax returns: Today, we take a look at what taxpayers should do if they receive a notice from the I NRI insurance: Benefits, risks, and features of GIFT City's dollar-denominated insurance plans. Global exposure, including oil and gold ETFs, and hedge against rupee depreciation are key draws of GIFT City ULIPs; but the market is nascent and experts caution against mixing insurance with investments. Can forming a HUF help you save tax? Experts explain who can create one, key benefits and what to consider. Forming a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) can help taxpayers manage taxes efficiently as it is a separate taxable entity under income tax law, however a family must understand how it works first. Here's what experts say. EPF Scheme 2026: Key changes in nominations, PF withdrawals, and employer compliance. Key features, such as the 12 percent contribution rate for employers and employees as well as the rupees 15,000 statutory wage ceiling, remain unchanged, but the formalisation of revised provisions will trigger new operational obligations. Gifting to your NRI child? Know the tax treatment of NRE deposits. Gifts from parents to NRI children are tax-free, while interest earned on NRE accounts and deposits is fully exempt from income tax for FEMA non-residents, unlike taxable NRO interest. Income tax return: How to report capital gains correctly to avoid ITR filing mistakes. Report even exempt equity gains under Section 112A, and file losses on time to carry them forward. EPFO: Here's how to update your KYC information online using the official portal , Stepwise guide. EPFO: Aadhaar linking and KYC updating is mandatory to avail several EPF services. Today, we check how EPF subscribers can update their KYC information online for their account using the retirement body's official portal. ITR filing: How Bitcoin, NFTs, airdrops, gifted crypto, and overseas wallets are taxed in India and how to report them. India's virtual digital asset taxation remains among the world's toughest. Investors face a flat thirty percent tax on gains and one percent TDS. Specific provisions govern the taxation of cryptocurrencies and non-fungible tokens. Airdrops and staking rewards are taxed as income upon receipt. Want to build wealth through property? First read these 2 'golden rules' from a financially independent millennial. Cody Berman, author of 'Retire by 30', used house hacking to achieve financial independence by investing in real estate. By renting part of his home, he reduced his housing costs and generated passive income. India versus US real estate: Transparency, legal protection, fraud risks, and what NRIs should consider before buying property. It is relatively easier to reliably buy or sell property in the United States, compared to the process in India, which can be fraught with delays and in some cases instances of fraud and malpractice. The liabilities and penalties for such instances are clearly stated and implementable in the US. ITR filing 2026: Secondary address field is now mandatory , here's how it can help taxpayers. The income tax department has mandated a secondary address field in all ITR forms for AY 2026-27, requiring additional communication details from taxpayers. Here's how it can help taxpayers. Portfolio allocation: Defensives lead in 2026; balanced portfolios keep pace with equities over the long run. The ideal mix depends on each investor's financial goals, risk appetite and time horizon, making asset allocation unique to every individual. In this week's TrendMap, ET Wealth compares seven asset combinations. Portfolios with gold have delivered a notable return boost in recent years. By Sameer Bhardwaj. 8th pay commission: Here's why NC-JCM wants employee levels combined , Pay scale merger proposal explained. The 8th pay commission: Meetings with employee groups is significant as their suggestions are expected to shape the panel's deliberations on remuneration, allowances, pay and pension structures for the central government workforce. RBI Governor flags Iran war, weak monsoon as the biggest risks to India's inflation and economic growth. Elevated inflation persists due to West Asian tensions and a weak monsoon outlook. The Reserve Bank of India maintains its policy rate, diverging from global peers. Governor Malhotra stated that domestic inflation pass-through has remained limited so far. India's economic fundamentals remain strong despite geopolitical uncertainties and growth forecasts. DA hike: Here's the full list of states that are considering Dearness Allowance increases for employees, pensioners. Dearness Allowance is the component of the basic salary periodically adjusted to counter increased cost-of-living due to inflation. Here's what we know about state governments' discussions on DA hikes. a India visa and passport services disrupted in Australia after Delhi High Court scraps government tender. The Delhi High Court has canceled a government tender for passport and visa services abroad. This decision came after losing bidders argued the selection process lacked fairness. Authorities must now seek fresh bids for services in four countries. Passport and visa services in Australia have already been suspended due to this dispute. Don't miss this tax benefit: How car buyers can claim a minimum rupees 10,000 refund. Car buyers may be eligible for an income tax refund due to 1 percent TCS collected on vehicle purchases exceeding rupees 10 lakh. This amount can be lower you tax liability or get you a refund if their tax payable is less than the TCS paid. Details here. US-based daughter goes to court against parents, seeking share in family land; Karnataka HC rejects her claim; here's why. Daughter married and settled in USA lost partition case in HC for getting share in family lands in Bengaluru for this reason; Know more. Know why did she lose the case in Karnataka High Court. Also know what does Hindu law say. TDS Return Filing 2026: Q1 Due on July 31, Check New Forms and Section Codes. Businesses and tax deductors must file Q1 TDS and TCS returns by July 31, 2026 using the new return forms and revised section codes under the Income-tax Act, 2025. Here's what has changed, the new forms to use, and the compliance steps to avoid filing errors. The F and O trap inside the barracks: How army officers are losing crores chasing the next trade. Stable salaries, easy access to credit and early trading wins are drawing serving and retired defence personnel into a cycle of leveraged bets, and mounting losses Can builder's loose papers lead to an income tax notice? ITAT explains the law. A Mumbai ITAT ruling has held that the Income Tax Department cannot tax a property buyer merely because their name appears in a builder's seized cash records. The Revenue must prove an undisclosed cash payment with independent corroborative evidence before invoking Section 69A. ITR filing 2026: When should a salaried taxpayer file ITR-2 instead of ITR-1?. Taxpayers must file accurate income tax returns using the correct ITR form to avoid being flagged for defects. The e-filing portal aids in selection. Here's when a salaried taxpayer needs to file ITR-2 instead of ITR-1. AMFI's Mutual Funds Sahi Hai shares a list of dos and don'ts for investors to avoid online frauds. AMFI's investor education initiative "Mutual Funds Sahi Hai" advised investors not to share sensitive information such as OTPs, passwords, and investment details with unauthorised distributors. Average active small-cap fund delivered 20.1 percent CAGR, with 16 percentage points lower drawdown than the benchmark. On average, active equity mutual funds have generated higher annualised returns while limiting losses better than their benchmark indices. An average active small-cap fund posted the strongest outperformance among the funds have been live since 2013. 'Adulting looks cheaper, but...': Pune woman shares monthly expense of family of 3: From rupees 31,000 rent to rupees 16,000 for nanny. A Pune woman shared monthly expenses of family of 3 living in Baner with two earning members. From rupees 31,000 rent to rupees 16,000 for nanny, let's have a look at the full list of expenditures.

transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. Is Zepto down? Users hit by mass outage, face disruptions on app and website. Zepto, the quick-commerce platform, appeared to suffer some outages on Sunday, with users reporting issues with its app and website, according to Downdetector. This unpronounceable series of glyphs is an incredible side project from Kieran Hebden (aka Four Tet). Just why? ʅ͡͡͡͡͡͡͡͡͡͡͡(̸̢̛̼̞̭͋ͅ)̸͚̰͛̔̾̀̿͒͂:̴͓̞̑̌̂̆̊͋̀:̸͎̟̯̂̓̌ ҉ ͡ ͞ ͞ ͞ ҉● ࿀ ● ࿀ ● ҉⃝ ⃝͢ ͞ ͘ ͞⃝̕ ͢ ̛ ⃝ ̸ ̡ ͢⃝̧ ͡ ͡ ̀ ̧ ̢⃝͜ ҉ ͞ ͞ ⃝͞ ͞ ͡⃝ ⃝҉҈҉҈҉҈҉҈҉҈҉ :̶̢͙͙͕̠̩͆(̷̮͍͚̫͚͂̍)̵̳̗̊( ̟̞̝̜̙̘̗̖҉̵̴̨̧̢̡̼̻̺̹̳̲̱̰̯̮̭̬̫̪̩̦̥ What am I supposed to do with this? It's un-Googleable. Unpronounceable. Netflix paid $587M for Ben Affleck's AI filmmaking startup. Netflix revealed that it paid $587 million in cash for InterPositive, a startup co-founded by Ben Affleck. The mysterious hum heard around the world may finally have an explanation. The mysterious sound known as The Hum has been reported worldwide for decades, yet its source often remains impossible to find. Researchers tested whether people who hear it have exceptional low-frequency hearing or measurable sounds produced inside their ears, but neither explanation fit most participants. The strongest possibility is that many cases involve low-frequency tinnitus, even though the sound seems completely external. Instagram, Facebook down? Thousands of users report mass outage; netizens say 'no posts coming up on feed after refresh'. Many users encountered error messages while attempting to log in to Facebook's desktop site. According to outage-tracking platform DownDetector, reports of issues with Instagram and Facebook occurred in New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Pune, Kolkata and other cities in India. Kodak EC35 is a dirt-cheap point-and-shoot film camera. Following the success of its $99 Kodak-branded Snapic A1, Reto Project is releasing the Kodak EC35, an even more affordable 35mm film camera for just $34.99. The EC35 certainly isn't fancy. Its 25mm acrylic lens with a fixed f/10 aperture and 1/100 shutter speed basically put it on par with a drugstore disposable. The fact What to watch for after Jensen Huang's Japan visit. Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem. Coffee may help the body fight stress and aging through a hidden cellular switch. Scientists may have uncovered one reason coffee is repeatedly associated with healthier aging and lower disease risk. Compounds in coffee appear to activate NR4A1, a receptor that helps protect cells from stress, inflammation, and damage. These protective effects disappeared when the receptor was removed, strengthening the connection. Surprisingly, plant-based compounds such as caffeic acid seemed far more active than caffeine itself. Google Pixel 11a key specs leaked ahead of Made by Google event: Tensor G6, 120Hz display and 8GB RAM tipped. A new leak about the Pixle 11a has showcased the phone's key specs including its display, processor and battery. I hate that I don't hate this song made with Suno. I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI's hardware plans?. On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public. Common antidepressant may ease long COVID's crushing fatigue. A low-cost antidepressant may offer new hope for people struggling with long COVID fatigue. In a randomized clinical trial involving 399 adults, fluvoxamine significantly reduced fatigue and improved quality of life compared with a placebo, making it one of the first medications to show meaningful benefits for this disabling condition. US allows federal employees to download TikTok on official devices. The US Justice Department has lifted the ban on TikTok for federal employees The FBI reportedly won't investigate ICE anymore. According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The Times. The reported change in guidance follows renewed scrutiny of violence by ICE agents who have killed two people in the TechCrunch Mobility: The battle over robotaxi rules. Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part. A tiny universe in a bottle reveals clues to the origins of life. Researchers have created cosmic dust from scratch by recreating space-like conditions inside glass tubes. The dust contains complex carbon-rich molecules built from elements essential to life and produces infrared signals similar to real material found in space. By studying these laboratory samples, scientists can explore how organic chemistry unfolds around stars and how comets, asteroids and meteorites may have carried those ingredients to Earth. Orchid is a delightfully retro and approachable hipster synth. In 2017, I bought an old Magnus chord organ off Craigslist for $10. It's one of my favorite music gear purchases. Electric chord organs let you play full chords with just a press of a button, making them perfect for aspiring singer-songwriters or fooling around at home. But chord organs largely fell out of favor 'Odyssey' director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious 'Trojan horse'. "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside." Birdfy's solar-powered smart feeder is down to one of its best prices. Birdfy has kicked off a midyear sale, taking up to 40 percent off a range of its smart bird feeders. One of the best deals is on Netvue's Birdfy Feeder AI-Powered Smart Bird Feeder with Camera, which has dropped to $149.99 at Birdfy and is its second-best price to date. The bundle, which includes a Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all. Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more. Scientists identify the rare meteorite that killed the dinosaurs 66 million years ago. The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs was likely an exceptionally rare CO chondrite from a distant region of the solar system. Its unusual chemistry suggests that planet-cooling dust and debris, rather than sulfur inside the asteroid, may have delivered the deadliest blow. US Marshals arrest the Tate brothers in Miami. The manosphere influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested Saturday in Miami by US Marshals in relation to new rape and sex trafficking charges in England. According to the Associated Press, British authorities are seeking the brothers' extradition. The new charges facing Andrew Tate include seven counts of rape, three of sex trafficking, three counts The Clapper was a bad smart home gadget , and a viral sensation. Clap on. Clap off. Well, more like, Clap, pause for half a beat but no longer because otherwise it'll stop hearing you, clap again because you waited too long, clap louder and faster, that didn't work, clap two more times, and suddenly: on. The Clapper didn't always work - and even when it did, it The future of physical games is not looking great. This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on video games and physical media, follow Jay Peters. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes on Sunday at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here. The grueling, 630-mile road race where the only fuel is sunlight. On July 19th, dozens of teams of high school students will begin a five-day, 630-mile road race from Fort Worth to Fort Stockton in Texas. But this is not your typical contest. The students design and build the cars themselves, using off-the-shelf parts and 3D printed materials. The winner is the team that accumulates the New AI blood test predicts heart disease 15 years early. A new AI-powered blood test could give people a remarkably early warning of serious heart and circulation problems. Developed by researchers at the University of Hong Kong, CardiOmicScore analyzes thousands of proteins and metabolites to estimate the risk of six major cardiovascular diseases, including heart attack, stroke, heart failure, and atrial fibrillation. Unlike genetic risk scores, which remain fixed throughout life, the system captures biological changes linked to a person's current health, lifestyle, and environment.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. 14 killed, many missing as flash floods, landslides ravage J and K's Poonch, Rajouri. Torrential rain caused flash floods and landslides in Poonch and Rajouri districts. At least fourteen people died, and several others remain missing after homes were buried. Rescue operations are underway for those trapped beneath debris and mud. Traffic on the Jammu-Srinagar NH-44 was disrupted, and the Amarnath Yatra was suspended. Bill seeks 3-year jail for disturbing rendition of 'Vande Mataram'. A new bill plans to jail individuals disrupting Vande Mataram singing. This proposed legislation aims to amend existing laws regarding national honor. Disrupting the national song could lead to three years imprisonment. The government cites historical significance for this proposed amendment. Monsoon session begins today as oppn gears up to corner Centre over temple row, NEET, E20. Cong can raise demand, can't force govt: Rijiju Farooq Abdullah meets Wangchuk's wife, wishes him good health. SRINAGAR: NC president Farooq Abdullah on Sunday met fasting climate activist Sonam Wangchuk's wife Gitanjali J. Government may seek Parliamentary nod for additional spending. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman may seek parliamentary approval for additional spending. Increased expenditure on fertiliser subsidies and electronics manufacturing initiatives is driving this need. GST and direct tax revenues remain on track, and DIPAM is pushing for additional realisations. The IDBI Bank transaction's finalisation could provide significant financial comfort. Even as fertility rates drop, young still aspire to partnership, parenthood, shows UN report. Young adults worldwide and in India still desire marriage and family life. Financial security and stable employment are crucial for partnership decisions. Many young people aspire to partnership and parenthood despite demographic shifts. India's youth express optimism about the future while worrying about economic insecurity. Less than half of 483 Lok Sabha MPs attended 90-100 percent of sittings in Parliament's budget session, shows analysis. Smaller parties demonstrated the highest average Member of Parliament attendance during the recent budget session. RLP and RLD achieved one hundred percent attendance for all thirty-one sittings. BJP and Congress, the largest parties, recorded lower average attendance figures. In Rajya Sabha, regional parties also led in parliamentary attendance percentages. 'We intend to work with PM Modi': Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar announces entry into NDA with 20 Mamata rebels. Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar announced her group's move to join the ruling NDA in Parliament. Twenty former TMC MPs are now aligning with the NCPI parliamentary group. This group intends to work with Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Amit Shah. They will support the government's initiatives and focus on constructive debate. 'No permission sought or granted': Delhi Police on CJP's Parliament march. Delhi Police has denied permission for a proposed march to Parliament on July 20. Prohibitory orders are in effect across the New Delhi district, banning assemblies. Unauthorized gatherings will face legal action under new Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita provisions. The Delhi High Court previously upheld the Centre's intervention in Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. 'People's safety our highest priority': Assam govt requests Army, Air Force to remain on standby for flood evacuations. NEW DELHI: With parts of Assam continuing to reel under floods, chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma on Sunday said the state government has requested the Army and the Air Force to remain on standby to assist in evacuating people from flood-hit districts. 'Stay here for the night': CJP's Abhijeet Dipke calls for night-long vigil ahead of 'Chalo Sansad' march. Friends! Disagreement in NDA? Bihar's Model Schools to be painted saffron, JD(U) raises concerns. While Opposition slams 'beginning of the saffronisation of education', BJP says choice of colour is not political but represents 'energy and positivity'. Ram Temple donation 'theft': SIT's final report ready, gives reform pointers. Standard operating procedures with regard to the counting room operations were not followed, and unqualified people were hired for operations. Proposal to bring Bihar colleges under government dept: Delhi University teachers warn against weakening structure. Delhi University Teachers Association members write to Governor Syed Ata Hasnain 'Suicide' of techie who left high-paying job: Police investigate as CCTV tracks her last moments. 25-year-old software engineer had recently moved back to Hyderabad from Bengaluru to live with her mother. Coming soon in Gujarat: A life-size Harappan town with markets, dockyards. Lothal is believed to have existed around 4,500 years ago, with excavations at the site revealing the world's oldest known artificial dock, connected to an old course of the Sabarmati river. Ayurveda doctor on bail in Gurugram cyber scam arrested again , in rupees 30-crore ponzi scheme. Case records show that the ac­c­used, Kitcha Veerbhadra Rao, was arrested on July 15 from Kadapa by the Andhra police for allegedly defrauding around 270 investors of over rupees 30 crore, about half of which has been returned. Ex-envoy Ajay Bisaria: 'Likelihood of something positive happening greater under this dispensation than any other... onus on Pakistan'. Bisaria was India's envoy to Pakistan during the Feb 2019 Pulwama terror attack and the Balakot aerial strikes, the capture and release of Indian Air Force fighter pilot Abhinandan Varthaman, and the abrogation of Art 370 in Kashmir. India chose to have its own bullet train as Japan could not have delivered E10 before 2034: Railway official. "The Japanese side has still not been able to share the offers for E10 trains, signalling system, and Operation Control Centre (OCC) systems because the train is still under design," said the official.

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And finally, here is what is happening around the world. Ferry carrying 133 passengers and crew sinks off Guyana coast. Fifteen children are among 67 people rescued, officials say, with dozens still missing. Health officials identify source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak. The US Food and Drug Administration says iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell and Walmart locations was the source of cyclosporiasis infections. Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US. He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison. Runners complete 'world's coolest marathon' on drifting North Pole ice. Organisers say 23 runners from seven countries took part in races at the Arctic's geographic North Pole. German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy. Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed. Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park. Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West. Watch: Torrential rain hits New York and New Jersey triggering flash floods. Water cascaded into subway stations and flooded streets, with people seen wading through water. Spain fans hail new generation, as Argentina says goodbye to an icon. Revellers take to streets of New York following Spain's defeat of Argentina in Messi's likely final World Cup match. São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election. Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa's least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state's elections, approximately 15 percent of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading... US bombs Iran for ninth consecutive night as Hormuz tensions escalate. Iranian media report blasts in cities in the far north and far south, including Tabriz and Chabahar.

transcript.title: Sign-off
transcript.text: That is your briefing. Stay sharp, and have a strong day.
